page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the instance delivers average multi-core processing capabilities, as evidenced by its mid-tier performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core tests. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 25% for single-core stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds for large uncached blocks, though the overall PassMark memory score falls into the lower quartile.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource ratio, the ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring stable, non-burstable multi-core performance without the premium of high-memory or GPU-accelerated instances. It is well-suited for applications such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, moderate database operations, and software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale machine learning inference, where its performance is constrained.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. Equipped with 48.0 GB of RAM, this KVM-based instance provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks position the instance in the average tier for multi-core operations, including software compilation, static web serving, and Redis database operations. However, single-core performance and memory scores are relatively weak, falling into the bottom quartile. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance offers a stable, non-burstable resource profile. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, moderate database operations, and general-purpose application hosting that do not demand high single-thread throughput or local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for stable multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg5.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, delivering stable multi-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core x86_64 instance offering balanced memory and stable multi-threaded performance for general-purpose application hosting.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 6149 vCPUs with 12 physical cores at 3.1 GHz
48.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average multi-core performance for database operations and web serving
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and software compilation
